Ukraine Conflict News





26 September 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Department of War Awards $33.5 Million to Increase Solid Rocket Motor Capacity and Capability DoD 26 Sep 2025 -- Today, the Department of War announced two Defense Production Act (DPA) Title III awards totaling $33.5 million to expand the solid rocket motor (SRM) industrial base.
  • U.S. Africa Command and Zambia CIV-MIL Partnership Brings Mobile Laboratories to Southern Africa: A Game Changer for Infectious Disease Outbreak Response AFRICOM 26 Sep 2025 -- In a significant boost to regional health security, three state-of-the-art mobile biological threat detection laboratories are arriving in Zambia and poised to modernize infectious disease outbreak response across nine southern African nations—Zambia, the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), Angola, Namibia, Botswana, Zimbabwe, Mozambique, Malawi, and Tanzania.
  • Multinational planners inject experimentation and innovation into African Lion 2026 Army News 26 Sep 2025 -- Military planners from U.S. Army Southern European Task Force, Africa (SETAF-AF), U.S. Africa Command (AFRICOM), Forze armate italiane [Italian Armed Forces] and Tunisian Armed Forces convened from Sept. 14-19, 2025, to initiate planning for African Lion 2026 (AL26), scheduled to take place in April across multiple African locations.
  • W.Va., Qatar Military Leaders Elevate Partnerships; NATO interoperability, global burden sharing, aviation platforms discussed CENTCOM 26 Sep 2025 -- West Virginia National Guard (WVNG) Adjutant General James "Jim" Seward traveled to Doha, Qatar, this week to meet with Qatar Armed Forces (QAF) counterparts as part of a series of key leader engagements meant to reinforce and elevate the relationships built between the militaries since their National Guard Bureau State Partnership Program (SPP) pairing was formalized in 2018.
  • Exercising at scale: What we're learning US Air Force 26 Sep 2025 -- Last month, the Department of the Air Force concluded its Department-Level Exercise series, a month-long series of exercises conducted across the Western Pacific, including Resolute Force Pacific, Resolute Space, Mobility Guardian 2025, Emerald Warrior and Bamboo Eagle 25-3.
  • CTF-66 showcases RAS capabilities with partners in Portugal-led unmanned systems demonstration during REPMUS/Dynamic Messenger 2025 US Navy 26 Sep 2025 -- Commander, Task Force (CTF) 66 conducted a live robotic and autonomous systems (RAS) demonstration with Allies and partners during the experimentation exercise Robotic Experimentation and Prototyping with Maritime Unmanned Systems (REPMUS) / Dynamic Messenger 2025, in Troia, Portugal, Sept. 25, 2025.
  • U.S. Navy to Christen Future USS Louis H. Wilson Jr. US Navy Press Release 26 Sep 2025 -- The U.S. Navy will christen the future USS Louis H. Wilson Jr. (DDG 126), during a ceremony at General Dynamics Bath Iron Works (BIW) on Saturday, September 27, at 10:30 a.m. (EST).BATH, Maine — The U.S. Navy will christen the future USS Louis H. Wilson Jr. (DDG 126), during a ceremony at General Dynamics Bath Iron Works (BIW) on Saturday, September 27, at 10:30 a.m. (EST).

  • NATO Secretary General visits New York during UNGA, addresses West Point cadets NATO 26 Sep 2025 -- On Wednesday (24 September), NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte travelled to New York as the United Nations hosted its 80th General Assembly, engaging with political leaders and senior officials.
  • The first dedicated dialogue on defence industrial cooperation and capabilities launched between NATO and the Republic of Korea NATO 26 Sep 2025 -- On Friday (26 September 2025), NATO Assistant Secretary General for Defence Industry, Innovation and Armaments, Ms Tarja Jaakkola, opened the first staff-to-staff dialogue on defence industrial cooperation and capabilities between NATO and the Republic of Korea. Ms Jaakkola and representatives of the Defence Acquisition Program Administration of the Republic of Korea discussed recent developments in the areas of defence industrial production and defence capabilities.

  • Thunder and lightning in the Top End skies Australia DOD 26 Sep 2025 -- F-35A Lightning II aircraft from 3 and 75 Squadrons took to the Northern Territory skies for Exercise Arnhem Thunder last month.
  • Army's new armour makes waves at sea Australia DOD 26 Sep 2025 -- A trial conducted with HMAS Adelaide this month has demonstrated Navy's ability to move some of Army's newest and heaviest land combat platforms.
  • Trilateral mortars on target Australia DOD 26 Sep 2025 -- In a landmark first, Australian Army soldiers joined their Japanese and United States counterparts in a combined mortar live-fire on Japanese soil, demonstrating the growing strength of trilateral defence cooperation during Exercise Orient Shield.

  • Minister Anand meets with Mark Rutte, NATO Secretary General Canada 26 Sep 2025 -- The Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, met with Mark Rutte, NATO Secretary General, on the margins of the UN General Assembly in New York City, New York.
  • Minister McGuinty introduces legislation to modernize the military justice system Canada 26 Sep 2025 -- Today, the Minister of National Defence, the Honourable David J. McGuinty, introduced An Act to amend the National Defence Act and other Acts (Military Justice System Modernization Act). The legislation was originally proposed as Bill C-66 but was terminated with the prorogation of Parliament in January 2025.
  • Reintroduction of the Military Justice System Modernization Act Canada 26 Sep 2025 -- Today, the Honourable David J. McGuinty, the Minister of National Defence, introduced An Act to amend the National Defence Act and other Acts (Military Justice System Modernization Act).

  • Mig-21 symbolises continuity of courage, discipline & patriotism that will inspire development of indigenous LCA-Tejas and upcoming AMCA: Raksha Mantri India PIB 26 Sep 2025 -- "The legacy of the MiG-21 will live on in India's pursuit of Aatmanirbharta in defence
  • INDIAN NAVY'S FIRST TRAINING SQUADRON MAKES PORT CALL AT MOMBASA, KENYA India PIB 26 Sep 2025 -- The ships of the Indian Navy's First Training Squadron (1TS) - INS Tir, INS Sujata, INS Shardul and ICGS Sarathi made a port call at Mombasa, Kenya on 25 Sep 2025.
  • NODAL TECHNOLOGY CENTRE SYMPOSIUM - 2025 India PIB 26 Sep 2025 -- Base Repair Depot, Palam organised the Nodal Technology Centre (NTC) Symposium-2025 with the theme of 'Indigenisation through Reverse Engineering Adopting Black Box Concept' on 23 September 2025 at New Delhi

  • Pakistan-Russia Joint Exercise Druzhba-VIII between Pakistan and Russian Armies is being conducted from 15-27 September 2025 in Counter Terrorism domain. ISPR Pakistan 26 Sep 2025

  • Intensifying threat looms large as UN highlights the world's growing nuclear arsenals UN News 26 Sep 2025 -- The UN Secretary General on Friday warned a high-level meeting in New York focused on ridding the world of nuclear weapons that the threat is only "accelerating and evolving".
  • Secretary-General's message on the International Day for the Total Elimination of Nuclear Weapons UN Secretary-General 26 Sep 2025
  • Secretary-General's remarks at the Plenary Meeting on the International Day for the Total Elimination of Nuclear Weapons [as delivered] UN Secretary-General 26 Sep 2025

  • EU Eyes 'Drone Wall' Amid Concerns Over Russian Airspace Violations RFE/RL 26 Sep 2025 -- Several European Union defense ministers are urgently holding talks on a "drone wall" after Russian drones allegedly violated Polish and Romanian airspace this month, and unauthorized drone incursions forced intermittent shutdowns at Danish and Norwegian airports.

  • Prime Minister Orpo highlights need for drone wall and calls for solidarity in the interest of security in meeting with Defence Commissioner Kubilius Finnish Government 26 Sep 2025 -- Prime Minister Petteri Orpo met for discussions with European Commissioner for Defence and Space Andrius Kubilius in Helsinki on Friday 26 September.

  • Signing of a memorandum of understanding on the establishment of an artillery ammunition production facility in Latvia Latvia MOD 26 Sep 2025 -- On Thursday, September 25, a memorandum was signed in Hamburg, Germany, on the construction of an artillery ammunition factory in Latvia. The memorandum was signed by Latvian Prime Minister Evika Siliņa, Minister of Economics Viktors Valainis, Ingrīda Ķirse, member of the board of State Defence Corporation LLC, and the investor, Rheinmetall AG, a leading German defence industry company.

  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Uzbekistan Foreign Minister Saidov US Dept. of State 26 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Kyrgyz Republic Foreign Minister Kulubaev US Dept. of State 26 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Uruguayan Foreign Minister Lubetkin US Dept. of State 26 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Nigerian Foreign Minister Tuggar US Dept. of State 26 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Foreign Minister Schialer US Dept. of State 26 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Salvadoran Foreign Minister Hill US Dept. of State 26 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Tajikistan Foreign Minister Muhriddin US Dept. of State 26 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Argentine Foreign Minister Werthein US Dept. of State 26 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Mongolian Foreign Minister Battsetseg US Dept. of State 26 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Mauritian Foreign Minister Ramful US Dept. of State 26 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Ecuadorian Foreign Minister Sommerfeld US Dept. of State 26 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Honduran Foreign Minister Bu US Dept. of State 26 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Burundian Foreign Minister Bizimana US Dept. of State 26 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Paraguayan Foreign Minister Ramírez US Dept. of State 26 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Singaporean Foreign Minister Balakrishnan US Dept. of State 26 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with South African Foreign Minister Lamola US Dept. of State 26 Sep 2025

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 26 September 2025 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 26 Sep 2025
  • Opening remarks by High Representative Kaja Kallas at the High-Level Meeting on Women, Peace and Security in the margins of the UN General Assembly European External Action Service (EEAS) 26 Sep 2025

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Prime Minister's Office Announcement Israel - Prime Minister 26 Sep 2025 -- In an unprecedented action, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, in a live broadcast from the UN building in New York, has announced that the IDF took control of the telephones of Gaza residents and Hamas members, and that his speech is now being broadcast live via the telephones.
  • Netanyahu hails Israel's 'stunning comeback' after 7 October attacks, vows to 'finish the job' UN News 26 Sep 2025 -- Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu told the UN General Assembly on Friday that Israel had rebounded from "its darkest day" to deliver "one of the most stunning military comebacks in history," declaring that his country will not rest until Hamas is eliminated and hostages are freed.

  • Gaza: Four more hospitals shut amid ongoing Israeli offensive UN News 26 Sep 2025 -- The escalating Israeli military offensive in Gaza City continues to overwhelm medical professionals there, with four more hospitals forced to shut down this month alone in the north of the war-torn enclave, the UN World Health Organization (WHO) said on Friday.
  • UNRWA Situation Report #189 on the Humanitarian Crisis in the Gaza Strip and the West Bank, including East Jerusalem UNRWA 26 Sep 2025
  • UN Human Rights Office updates database of businesses involved in Israeli settlements in occupied West Bank OHCHR 26 Sep 2025 -- The UN Human Rights Office today issued an update to its database of businesses involved in certain activities in illegal Israeli settlements in the occupied Palestinian territory, listing a total of 158 business enterprises from 11 countries.

  • Launch of the Emergency Coalition for the Financial Sustainability of the Palestinian Authority Government of Norway 26 Sep 2025
  • Statement on protection of journalists in Gaza Canada 26 Sep 2025
  • Launch of the Emergency Coalition for the Financial Sustainability of the Palestinian Authority Spain MFA 26 Sep 2025 -- ​Today, Belgium, Denmark, France, Iceland, Ireland, Japan, Norway, Saudi Arabia, Slovenia, Spain, Switzerland and the United Kingdom announced the launch of the Emergency Coalition for the Financial Sustainability of the Palestinian Authority.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, Ukraine UNOCHA 26 Sep 2025

  • Special Envoy on Afghan Affairs of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Yue Xiaoyong Represents China at the Fourth Informal Meeting on Afghanistan Between Foreign Ministers of China, Russia, Pakistan and Iran PRC MND 26 Sep 2025
  • Afghans Fear Losing 'Last Hope' As Taliban Shuts Down Internet RFE/RL 26 Sep 2025 -- Maryam has worked as an online interpreter since the Taliban banned women from most jobs in Afghanistan.
  • Press release on the ministerial meeting of the regional quartet on Afghanistan Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Sep 2025

  • CENTCOM Urges Nations to Repatriate Displaced and Detained People from Syria CENTCOM 26 Sep 2025 -- During a United Nations conference Sept. 26 on camps and places of detention in northeast Syria, the commander of U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) called on countries to repatriate their citizens.
  • UN warns time running out for families with terrorist ties stranded in Syria camps UN News 26 Sep 2025 -- Six years after the defeat of ISIL, also known as Daesh, tens of thousands of people with alleged or actual ties to the terrorist group remain detained in camps in northeast Syria in dire conditions that continue to worsen.

  • UN expert calls on Mali to remain in the International Criminal Court OHCHR 26 Sep 2025 -- A UN expert today urged Mali to reconsider its plan to withdraw from the Rome Statute, the founding treaty of the International Criminal Court (ICC), warning of serious consequences.

  • South Sudan: Türk alarmed by deteriorating human rights situation amid rising violence and political tensions OHCHR 26 Sep 2025 -- UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk on Friday expressed alarm at the fast-deteriorating human rights situation in South Sudan, following credible reports that almost 2,000 civilians have been killed this year alone as political tensions and hostilities have escalated.

  • Mr. Massad Boulos, Senior Advisor for Africa to the President of the United States, reaffirmed, on Friday, Washington's support for Morocco's sovereignty over its Sahara and the Moroccan Autonomy Plan as "the only basis for a just and lasting resolution" to the regional conflict. Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Sep 2025

  • Security Council Committee Established Pursuant to Resolution 2653 (2022) Concerning Haiti Discusses Panel of Experts' Final Report UN Security Council 26 Sep 2025

News Reports

  • Meeting with elected heads of regions President of Russia 26 Sep 2025 -- The President held a videoconference meeting with the heads of 21 regions of the Russian Federation who were elected during the single voting day on September 14, 2025.
  • Meeting with Chairperson of the Central Election Commission Ella Pamfilova President of Russia 26 Sep 2025 -- Last night, Vladimir Putin held a meeting with Chairperson of the Central Election Commission Ella Pamfilova in the Kremlin to discuss the results of the single voting day in 2025.
  • Meeting with Prime Minister of Armenia Nikol Pashinyan President of Russia 26 Sep 2025 -- Vladimir Putin held a meeting with Prime Minister of Armenia Nikol Pashinyan at the Kremlin.
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's participation in BRICS foreign ministers' meeting Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Sep 2025
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Prime Minister of Saint Vincent and the Grenadines Ralph Goncalves Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Sep 2025
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation of Cambodia Prak Sokhonn Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Sep 2025
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Kingdom of Thailand Sihasak Phuangketkeow Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Sep 2025
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Chairman of the Presidential Leadership Council of the Republic of Yemen Rashad Al-Alimi Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Sep 2025
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs and Expatriates of Jordan Ayman Safadi Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Sep 2025
  • Deputy Foreign Minister Sergey Vershinin's remarks at the inaugural session of the Global Dialogue on AI Governance, New York, September 25, 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Sep 2025
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with UN Secretary-General António Guterres Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Sep 2025
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of South Sudan Monday Semaya Kumba Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Sep 2025
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with OSCE Secretary General Feridun Sinirlioğlu Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Sep 2025

  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Minister of External Affairs of the Republic of India Subrahmanyam Jaishankar Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Sep 2025
  • Transcript of Weekly Media Briefing by the Official Spokesperson (September 26, 2025) India MEA 26 Sep 2025

  • Ding Xuexiang and Russian Deputy Prime Minister Alexander Novak Co-chair the 22nd Meeting of the China-Russia Energy Cooperation Committee PRC MND 26 Sep 2025.
  • Vice Foreign Minister Ma Zhaoxu Meets with U.S. Deputy Secretary of State Christopher Landau PRC MND 26 Sep 2025.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on September 26, 2025 PRC MofA 26 Sep 2025
  • China's Premier Li calls for solidarity, peace and shared economic prosperity in UN address UN News 26 Sep 2025 -- Chinese Premier Li Qiang on Friday marked the UN's 80th anniversary with a call for world leaders to defend multilateralism, reject "Cold War" thinking and step up cooperation in the face of global turmoil.

  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 26 Sep 2025

  • UN To Reimpose Global Sanctions On Iran. What Are They And How Will Tehran React? RFE/RL 26 Sep 2025 -- The United Nations is set to reimpose wide-ranging sanctions on Iran over Tehran's disputed nuclear program.
  • Imprisoned Iranian Activist Rashidi Dies Amid Allegations Of Medical Neglect RFE/RL 26 Sep 2025 -- Iranian activist Somayeh Rashidi, labeled as a political prisoner by multiple groups, has died 10 days after suffering seizures in a prison south of Tehran, leading cellmates and rights organizations to accuse authorities of failing to provide necessary care for the woman.
  • Iran's nuclear escalation is a threat to international peace and security: UK Explanation of Vote at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 26 Sep 2025 -- Statement by Ambassador Barbara Woodward, UK Permanent Representative to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Iran.
  • UN Security Council blocks China-Russia resolution on Iran sanctions UN News 26 Sep 2025 -- A resolution to extend sanctions relief to Iran under a 2015 nuclear deal failed to pass in the UN Security Council on Friday.
  • Security Council Fails to Adopt Resolution Extending Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action on Iran's Nuclear Programme UN Security Council 26 Sep 2025 -- The Security Council today failed to adopt a resolution that would have extended United Nations sanctions relief under the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action, following the decision by France, Germany and the United Kingdom to trigger the reimposition of sanctions — the so-called "snapback" mechanism — citing Iran's significant non-performance of its commitments under the Plan.

  • Foreign Ministry statement on retaliatory personal sanctions against representatives of the UK political establishment and expert community Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Sep 2025
  • The United Kingdom does not stand up for one nation's rights, whilst ignoring others. But acts resolutely, with the UN Charter as our compass: UK National Statement at the UN General Assembly UK FCDO 26 Sep 2025 -- Statement by The Rt Hon David Lammy, Lord Chancellor and Secretary of State for Justice and Deputy Prime Minister, at the UN General Assembly General Debate of the 80th Session
  • PM meeting with Prime Minister Carney of Canada: 26 September 2025 Gov.UK 26 Sep 2025
  • PM meeting with Prime Minister Albanese of Australia: 26 September 2025 Gov.UK 26 Sep 2025

  • Pakistan's Prime Minister warns of regional tensions, calls for ceasefire in Gaza UN News 26 Sep 2025 -- Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif told the United Nations General Assembly on Friday that the world faces "testing times" marked by conflict, terrorism, and climate change, while urging urgent action for peace in South Asia, the Middle East and beyond.

  • Foreign Ministry statement on retaliatory personal sanctions against representatives of the UK political establishment and expert community Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Sep 2025

  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Kazakhstan Deputy Prime Minister-Foreign Minister Nurtleu US Dept. of State 26 Sep 2025

  • PM call with President Erdoğan of Türkiye: 26 September 2025 Gov.UK 26 Sep 2025

  • Norway's Statement in the General Debate of the 80th General Assembly Government of Norway 26 Sep 2025

  • Speech by Maria Malmer Stenergard at UNGA80 Government Offices of Sweden 26 Sep 2025

  • Rearmament without strengthening the welfare state is the wrong path Republic of Slovenia 26 Sep 2025 -- Slovenia has been a NATO member for more than two decades, yet debates about defense spending and the role of the Alliance remain lively.

  • We must all keep alive the principles of multilateralism: UK Statement at the G20 Foreign Ministers' meeting. UK FCDO 26 Sep 2025 -- Statement by The Rt Hon Yvette Cooper, Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, at the G20 Foreign Ministers' Meeting.

  • Prime Minister of Barbados calls for defence of rules-based world order UN News 26 Sep 2025 -- Prime Minister Mia Mottley of Barbados on Friday told world leaders the survival of small States and global peace depends on preserving a rules-based international system.

  • World News in Brief: New declaration on NCDs and mental health, Khartoum shelter crisis, WFP lifeline in Ukraine, South Sudan rights update UN News 26 Sep 2025 -- World leaders have thrown their weight behind the first-ever United Nations global political declaration tackling noncommunicable diseases (NCDs) and mental health together.

  • Prime Minister of Barbados calls for defence of rules-based world order UN News 26 Sep 2025 -- Prime Minister Mia Mottley of Barbados on Friday told world leaders the survival of small States and global peace depends on preserving a rules-based international system.
